channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Fourth S-400 System En Route to India, Set for Deployment Along Pakistan Border
item.description: India is set to receive its fourth S-400 Triumf air defence system from Russia, with delivery expected by mid-May following completion of pre-dispatch inspections by Indian Air Force (IAF) officials in April 2026. The fifth and final unit under the contract is expected later this year, bringing the full complement of five S-400 squadrons into service.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Flying Wedge Confirms Thailand as First Export Customer for Kaalbhairav ISR UAV, Deliveries Await Certification
item.description: Indian defence startup Flying Wedge Defence and Aerospace (FWDA) has confirmed that Thailand will be the first export customer for its FWD 100 ISR autonomous aircraft, a platform that now appears to be rebranded under the Kaalbhairav family. The development marks a notable milestone for India’s private UAV sector, as the company had earlier indicated in February 2025 that it had secured a global export order from a Sub-Saharan nation, without disclosing the buyer at the time.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US-Mandated Surveillance in F-16 Hangars Under Spotlight After Operation Sindoor Strikes
item.description: Amid ongoing diplomatic and military tensions between India and Pakistan, new details have emerged highlighting the extent of American oversight on Pakistan’s F-16 fleet, raising fresh scrutiny about aircraft losses claimed during India’s Operation Sindoor in May 2025. Indian officials and defence observers point to US-installed monitoring infrastructure, including live CCTV and technical security systems inside dedicated F-16 facilities, as potential sources of definitive evidence regarding damage sustained at key airbases.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rafale–Super Sukhoi Split Strategy: How the IAF Can Maximize Combat Power Without Crippling Its Budget
item.description: The Indian Air Force’s force-structure dilemma in 2026 is no longer just about numbers—it is about how to allocate finite capital across competing priorities: immediate capability gaps, long-term self-reliance, and the transition to fifth-generation systems. In that context, the “split strategy” of combining a limited direct purchase of Rafale F4 fighters with a comprehensive “Super Sukhoi” upgrade for the Su-30MKI fleet is emerging as a fiscally disciplined and operationally coherent pathway. It attempts to reconcile two competing imperatives: acquiring high-end, combat-ready platforms quickly while extracting maximum value from an already large legacy fleet.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Spacetech Startups Pivot to Defence as Geopolitics and Faster Payments Outweigh Commercial Markets
item.description: Bengaluru’s space technology startups are increasingly shifting their focus from purely commercial applications to defence and national security projects. Rising geopolitical tensions, quicker government payments, and the promise of large-scale contracts are driving this strategic realignment, even as founders acknowledge the tough choices involved in working with the defence sector.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: TRIYAM-3D AI Air Defence System Unveiled: India’s Autonomous Gatling Gun Solution for Drone and Swarm Threats
item.description: BSS – Bharat Supply & Support Alliance has unveiled its TRIYAM-3D “Detect | Decide | Destroy” system, an AI-driven autonomous air defence solution specifically engineered to counter the rapidly evolving threat of drones and drone swarms. The system integrates detection, decision-making, and hard-kill engagement into a single compact architecture, marking a significant step forward in India’s indigenous counter-UAS capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: SSS Defence Unveils Advanced T12 12-Gauge Semi-Auto Shotgun with Drop-In Trigger for Military and CUAS Roles
item.description: SSS Defence has introduced a new 12-gauge semi-automatic shotgun designed specifically for modern military requirements, combining rugged construction, innovative features, and exceptional versatility. The gas-operated, magazine-fed T12 stands out as one of the most advanced tactical shotguns currently available, particularly due to its unique “drop-in” trigger system — believed to be the only such feature in a military-grade shotgun globally.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Armenia discuss avenues for joint ventures in military hardware development
item.description: Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) General Anil Chauhan held a meeting with Armenia’s Chief of the General Staff Lt General Edvard Asryan in New Delhi on Tuesday, discussing ways to have joint ventures in the development of military hardware.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Op Sindoor showed terrorism centres no longer immune, says Rajnath Singh at Kyrgyzstan SCO meet
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Tuesday addressed the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Bishkek, stressing the urgent need for a collective and uncompromising approach to tackle the “evils” of terrorism, separatism and extremism by dismantling safe havens and rejecting any political justification for such acts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Munir Gets Desperate As Pakistan’s Relevance Fades After Failed US-Iran Talks: Report
item.description: With the second round of US-Iran talks to be held in Pakistan faltering, frustration is growing in Islamabad, which has remained under a virtual lockdown for nearly a week amid heightened security and preparations for the foreign delegations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Who Was LeT Operative Sheikh Yousuf Afridi, Close Aide Of Hafiz Saeed Killed In Pak
item.description: Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) commander Sheikh Yousuf Afridi, described as a close associate of Hafiz Saeed, was shot dead in Pakistan’s Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province by unidentified gunmen in what investigators are treating as a targeted killing, adding to a pattern of attacks on militants in the country since 2023.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Gangster Dawood Ibrahim’s Aide Salim Dola Deported To India, Will Be Handed Over To NCB
item.description: In a significant breakthrough for Indian law enforcement and intelligence agencies, Salim Dola, a close aide of fugitive gangster Dawood Ibrahim and a key figure in an international drug syndicate, was deported on Tuesday. The operation marks a major step in the country’s ongoing crackdown on transnational narcotics networks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Mumbai Stabbing Horror: Guard Recounts Brutal ‘Religious Identity’ Assault
item.description: Subroto Sen, a security guard who was stabbed in a violent assault by 31-year-old Zaib Zuber Ansari in Mumbai’s Naya Nagar, has narrated to the police the sequence of events from the horrifying night that left him and his supervisor, Rajesh Mishra, seriously injured.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: EAM Jaishankar And UNGA President Discuss Reformed Multilateralism, Sdgs And West Asia Conflict
item.description: External Affairs Minister (EAM) S Jaishankar held a meeting with United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) President Annalena Baerbock in New Delhi on Tuesday, discussing UN80, sustainable development goals (SDGs), Artificial Intelligence (AI) implications and the ongoing West Asia conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Restoration of Article 370 remains core agenda of National Conference: Farooq Abdullah
item.description: National Conference supremo Farooq Abdullah on Monday said the restoration of Article 370 remains the core agenda of the party, and “there is no question of stepping back from this struggle”. He also said that the dual government system in Jammu and Kashmir is not detrimental to the growth of the Union territory.
item.dc:creator: admin
